name: mining
description: /mining — lens-driven divergence + pruning workflow as a slash command. Sibling to /domain: where <NAME>.md is the snapshot + <NAME>.log.md is the step log, mining adds the THIRD pillar — <NAME>.mining.md (cycle-by-cycle lens-driven analysis tree) + <NAME>.mining.tape (idea cart of @X entries). A 'cycle' = one round of applying a single lens to the active branch frontier, recording new leaves (ideas / sub-claims / sub-branches), and either continuing the cycle (`/mining append`), opening a new cycle (`/mining cycle new`), or declaring the lens depleted (`/mining depletion` — 0 new leaves under the current lens). Bundled lens catalogue (extensible via `~/.sidecar/lens/<name>.md`): **same-formula** (when two systems share the same math, equivalent mechanism lurks beneath the surface domain difference) · **ouroboros** (X referencing itself → fixed-point / self-closure surfaces; ouroboros surfacing is a self-referential signal that auto-closes the goal) · **dimensional** (dimensional ladder — quantities of the same dimension are convertible across abstraction levels) · **tension** (contradiction mining — two premises in conflict → branch fork) · **combinatorial** (A × B orthogonal product set exploration) · **custom** (free-form user-supplied lens). Verbs: bare `/mining` shows active-domain mining status (cycle count · leaf count · undepleted lenses); `/mining <lens>` runs a lens round on the current frontier; `/mining append <text>` appends a timestamped entry to the current cycle; `/mining cycle new <title>` opens a new cycle header + skeleton; `/mining depletion` marks the current lens as depleted (closes the cycle); `/mining tree` renders the cumulative ASCII branch tree. The active-domain scope is read from the `domain` skill's active pointer (commons @D g58 — off-domain mining forbidden; `/domain set <NAME>` first if none active). Cycles are chronological + append-only; depletion closes a cycle, not the file. Derived from demiurge RTSC's 13-cycle organic emergence (Cooper-Kramers fixed-point LL-1 + Yoneda equivalence LL-2 discovered via this exact pattern). NL triggers — '/mining', '/mining <lens>', 'mining cycle', 'lens 발산', 'lens 채굴', 'divergence cycle', 'lens 적용', 'mining tree'.
